title: Theme
description: A visual theme for NoteTaker. Stored as an array in themes.json inside the user's storage directory. Each theme overrides a subset of the app's CSS custom properties via the `tokens` map.

schemaVersion.description:
Schema version for this theme object. Omitting this field is equivalent to version 1. The app automatically migrates older themes by filling any missing tokens from the mode defaults (light or dark), then stamps the current version.

Version history:
  1 — initial shape: 22 tokens covering bg/text/border/accent/state/shadow.
  2 — added --transition-slow token; ALL_TOKEN_KEYS now covers radius, layout, motion and font tokens so they are properly cleared on theme switch.

id.description: Unique, stable identifier. Use kebab-case. Built-in themes use the prefix 'built-in-'.

name.description: Human-readable display name shown in the theme picker.

mode.description: Whether this theme targets a light or dark base. The app switches CSS variable defaults based on this value before applying `tokens`. Missing tokens are filled from the matching mode defaults.

isBuiltIn.description: When true the theme ships with the app and cannot be deleted by the user.

description.description: One-sentence description shown below the theme name in the picker.

tokens.description: Map of CSS custom property names to values. Only the properties you want to override need to be present — all others fall back to the mode defaults. Missing tokens are filled automatically during migration.

additionalProperties.description: Any valid CSS value (hex, rgb, hsl, var(), etc.)

--bg-secondary.description: Secondary surfaces — toolbar, footer, inner panels.

--bg-tertiary.description: Tertiary surfaces — code blocks, chip backgrounds, deeply nested items.

--bg-hover.description: Background applied to interactive elements on hover.

--text-secondary.description: Subdued text — note titles in the list, metadata labels.

--text-muted.description: Faint text — placeholders, timestamps, keyboard hints.

--border-strong.description: Heavier border used for emphasis or focus rings.

--accent.description: Primary brand / action color. Used for buttons, links, active states.

--accent-hover.description: Accent color when the pointer hovers over an accented element.

--accent-fg.description: Foreground (text/icon) color painted on top of an accent-colored surface. Usually white.

--accent-soft.description: Low-opacity tint of --accent for selection backgrounds, focus rings and the todo add-row hover. Typically accent at 10–15% opacity.

--danger.description: Color for destructive actions (delete, error states).

--success.description: Positive / completed state color. Also used for todo task completion checkboxes and step progress bars.

--shadow-xs.description: Extra-small box-shadow for subtle lift (note cards at rest).

--shadow-sm.description: Small box-shadow (note cards on hover, todo item cards, dropdowns).

--radius-md.description: Medium border-radius. Used for todo item cards and the detail panel sections.

--sidebar-width.description: Pixel width of the sidebar panel.

--transition-slow.description: Slower transition for larger motions (modals, panel slides).

--font.description: Font stack for all UI text.

--font-mono.description: Monospace font stack for code blocks and timestamps.

examples.name: Ocean Dark
examples.description: Deep ocean blues with a teal accent — easy on the eyes for long sessions.
examples._comment: Tokens not listed here (radius, transition, font, etc.) are automatically filled from the dark-mode defaults during migration.

examples.name: Rose Light
examples.description: Warm rose tones with a clean white canvas.
